title: Why Did BioNTech SE (BNTX) Stock Fall -22% Today?
author: tickeron_community
selftext: BioNTech SE ([BNTX](https://tickeron.com/ticker/BNTX/)) is a Mainz, Germany-based biotechnology company best known for co-developing the mRNA-based COVID-19 vaccine with Pfizer ([PFE](https://tickeron.com/ticker/PFE/)) and for its expanding oncology pipeline. Shares plunged approximately 22% on Tuesday, March 10, 2026, falling from a prior close of $102.16 to trade near $79–$80 intraday — the stock's steepest single-session decline in recent memory. The selloff was driven by a combination of a Q4 2025 earnings miss on the bottom line, deeply disappointing 2026 revenue guidance, and the stunning announcement that both co-founders would be stepping down by year-end.
